metadata.title:
CLAMP LID ON


SCALER JOINT

BREWERY LICENSE PLAN OF
RUNNING SALOONS AFFORDS
MEANS OF CLOSING PLACE
IN SPITE OF INJUNCTION.

pages.raw_text:
The corporation counsel's Office
closed the Scaler saloon this morn
ing on a charge that it was being
operated without license. P. c.
Kaylor, Scaler's man in charge
was arrested on warrant sworn
to by Detective Burns and held un
der 5000 bonds. Although the coun
ell was prevented from hearing the
revocation of license charges last
night. it is held that the place can
be kept closed indefinitely by the
rule under which today's action
was taken.


Enough fault was found or al
legged in Corporation Counsel
Aliens proceedings against Scaler
upon which to secure a temporary
restraining order that prevented
the city council from hearing the
charges.


The Spokane Brewing & Malt
ing Co is making a fight to retain
the license. The license is issued
In the name of Henry Bosnian,
who serves the brewery in the ca
pacity of license holder.


The injunction hearing is set foI
May before Judge Hinkle, who
issued the restraining order The
council proceedings were post
poned until May 10.


The principle allegation made in
support of the injunction against
the council is that proper recon-
mendation in the matter of revok-
ing license had not been made
and the procedure prescribed by
ordinance not complied with in
other respects. 1t is contended
that Allen secured signatures OF
the city commissioners to the
charges as individuals instead of
as police commission board.


1t is now understood that the
brewing company claims that the
license is the property of the
Kirkwood distillery and the cor
poration counsel's Office is making
an effort to ascertain the actual
owner.


The complaint against the resort
includes the selling of liquor to
young girls. Both Scaler and
Kearns have been heavily fined in
the justice of peace court on this
charge.
